mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equities through lower discount rates and improved liquidity. When the policy rate is falling, S&P 500 forward returns over the next month should be positive, with the signal strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during falling-FEDFUNDS periods, or if the sign flips and negative returns dominate when FEDFUNDS declines while CPIAUCSL is stable or easing.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure corporate revenues and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is not offsetting the labor market deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with rising UNRATE do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if PAYEMS growth neutralizes the signal and returns remain positive in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumption and housing demand, supporting revenue expectations and cyclical sentiment. This joint demand impulse should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days more than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal has no incremental predictive power over single-variable effects, or if positive joint demand readings are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO without a matching rise in PAYEMS signals improving productivity and capacity utilization, which should support margins and raise equity returns over the next quarter. This is a supply-side positive only when output growth outpaces labor growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises faster than PAYEMS over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes of faster INDPRO than PAYEMS do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when production gains are accompanied by proportional labor gains.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in GDPC1 identifies broad final-demand weakness that should lower expected corporate earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest after confirmed quarterly GDP deceleration rather than one-off monthly noise.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates for 2 consecutive quarterly observations
failure_condition: Reject if confirmed GDP deceleration is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the sign is not negative after controlling for normal quarter-to-quarter noise.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold, high rates compress valuation multiples and tighten credit, so rising CPIAUCSL should coincide with weaker S&P 500 performance over the next month. The adverse effect should be most visible once policy is already restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate episodes with rising CPIAUCSL do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship weakens materially when FEDFUNDS is below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend while UNRATE rises indicates labor-market deterioration that should hurt equity returns quickly through weaker wages, spending, and sentiment. The signal should be negative over the next week when the labor pair deteriorates together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises within the same month
failure_condition: Reject if coincident PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the effect is absent when both labor signals move in the same adverse direction.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-led recovery, rising HOUST should precede better equity returns by signaling future construction activity, consumer durable demand, and financing demand. The effect is positive over the next month only when housing starts are trending higher from a low base.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior 6-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start rebounds after a prior decline do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal remains absent despite repeated recoveries from low levels.
